mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding real output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rising periods show negative median 60-day returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and forward returns is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals weakening residential investment and broader economic softness, which depresses equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: If HOUST below 1600 does not lead to negative median 20-day returns, or the false positive rate (negative signal but positive returns) exceeds 50% over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below CPI inflation, real interest rates become negative, compressing the equity risk premium and incentivizing risk-taking, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If Rank IC of the negative real rate signal is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or if the hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in nonfarm payroll growth during a tight labor market (low unemployment) signals emerging economic weakness that often precedes equity market declines.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS slowdown when UNRATE<5% does not produce significantly negative 20-day returns compared to non-slowdown periods (t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real retail sales growth reflects robust consumer spending, a key driver of corporate revenues and earnings, which lifts equity prices over the short to medium term.
failure_condition: If RSAFS positive growth periods show negative median 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if the directional accuracy of the signal is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above 3% year-on-year signals above-trend expansion that boosts corporate profits and drives positive equity returns over a quarter ahead.
failure_condition: If GDP growth >3% does not lead to positive median 60-day returns over any 5-year window, or if the average return difference from other periods is not economically significant (>1%).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and payroll employment jointly indicate a broad demand contraction that historically predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM decline > 2% and PAYEMS MoM change < 0
failure_condition: If the combined signal does not achieve a higher negative hit rate than either single-series signal alone over 10-year rolling windows, or if the mean return difference is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-on-month industrial production growth in the early expansion phase (positive but below-trend GDP growth) confirms robust manufacturing momentum, supporting positive equity returns in the near term.
regime_hint: early expansion, GDPC1 YoY growth positive but below 3%
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth >0.5% in early expansion does not yield positive median 20-day returns, or if the signal generates more false positives than true positives (accuracy < 0.6) over any 3-year window.
